content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can leverage adaptive memory compression that prioritizes retention of unexpected, curiosity-triggering experiences over routine mundane observations, creating a self-reinforcing cycle that improves long-term task performance. This compression framework automatically prunes low-curiosity routine memory to make space for exploring new high-reward unknowns, aligning memory capacity with the agent's intrinsic motivation.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
